findings.current_version: specification-based (no semantic versioning - stable, language-agnostic blueprint)
findings.summary: Attractor is a specification (not a code library) defining a DOT-based pipeline orchestration system. The specification includes 5-phase lifecycle (PARSE/VALIDATE/INITIALIZE/EXECUTE/FINALIZE), handler types (codergen, wait.human, conditional, parallel, manager_loop), and state management patterns. The specification is stable with no breaking changes documented.
sd_sections_updated: Section 2.1 (Baton-Passing Concept) - added Attractor Spec alignment to ManagerLoopHandler pattern
Section 2.3 (Independent Validation Worker) - added Attractor CodergenBackend and holdout scenario alignment
New Section 5 (Validation Findings & Gotchas) - added research findings with 5 key insights

sd_changes_summary: Added Attractor Spec alignment annotations to baton-passing and independent validation patterns, plus validation findings section documenting spec quality criticality, agent reliability on edge cases, and subprocess coordination patterns.
gotchas: Spec quality is critical bottleneck - validation node's acceptance criteria must be precise and unambiguous or validator will struggle
Agent reliability on edge cases - validation workers may miss novel scenarios not seen during spec writing; mitigation is exhaustive acceptance criteria
Independent validation worker serves role similar to StrongDM's Digital Twin Universe - requires good instrumentation (git diff, test output, metrics) to be effective
No pattern-specific gotchas for baton-passing, independent validation, or subprocess spawning - patterns are stable and used in community implementations

validation_status.spec_alignment: PASS - All patterns align with Attractor Spec Section 4.5 (CodergenBackend) and 4.11 (ManagerLoopHandler)
validation_status.implementation_patterns: PASS - Independent validation worker, baton-passing observe/steer/wait, subprocess spawning are all validated patterns
validation_status.breaking_changes: NONE - No breaking changes detected in Attractor specification

research_sources.context7_search: No direct match for strongdm/attractor in Context7 library registry - framework is specification-only, not indexed as package
research_sources.perplexity_research: Confirmed Attractor is specification (github.com/strongdm/attractor with 3 markdown files, ~6000-7000 lines), no executable code. StrongDM published Feb 2026. Community implementations in Rust (streamweave-attractor), Go, Python, TypeScript, Ruby, etc.
research_sources.local_specification: Accessed /Users/theb/Documents/Windsurf/claude-harness-setup/.claude/documentation/attractor-spec-reference.md which contains full specification with sections 1-11. Validated sections 4.5 (CodergenBackend) and 4.11 (ManagerLoopHandler) match SD references exactly.
research_sources.gap_analysis: Consulted /Users/theb/Documents/Windsurf/claude-harness-setup/docs/research/attractor-spec-analysis.md which provides context on project's current Attractor maturity (Level 3 with Level 4 characteristics) and identified gaps

validation_notes: Pattern aligns with ManagerLoopHandler observe/steer/wait cycle - no modifications needed
Independent validation worker extends base spec with StrongDM Level 4 philosophy: 'validation replaces code review'
Read-only tool restriction on validator enforces blind review discipline
Subprocess spawning via CodergenBackend.run() interface is stable pattern across community implementations
Signal-based coordination (files, PIDs, git logs) matches patterns in streamweave-attractor reference implementation
